name: Piemento Cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the piementos.
2. Grate the Velveeta cheese into a large bowl.
3. Add the piementos, black pepper, and red pepper to the mayonnaise in a separate bowl.
4. Mix well until combined.
5. Combine the mayonnaise mixture with the grated Velveeta cheese.
6. Let sit at room temperature for about half an hour.
7. Store in the refrigerator in a sealed jar.
Notes:
Try using Velveeta first, then substitute if necessary. I find that Velveeta gives a better consistency. It is recommended to hand-grate rather than use a food processor, as processors can liquify the mixture. Occasionally, 1 tsp ground mustard can be substituted for the red pepper (if desired). This spread works well on crackers or sandwiches and also makes an excellent burger topping. It will keep in the refrigerator for at least one month.
